ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) — IIS w3wp.exe spawning native tools (ProxyLogon webshell execution chain)
ucs.description: Hunts the signature post-exploitation pattern that defined Silk Typhoon's ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/-26857/-26858/-27065) campaign and continues into 2024-25 Exchange-server intrusions: the IIS worker process w3wp.exe (Exchange app pool) launching cmd.exe, PowerShell, net.exe, procdump or LOLBins. This is the in-memory execution path of a China Chopper / 'gif' style webshell dropped after the SSRF auth bypass.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" AND Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","ipconfig.exe","systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe") by host Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process Processes.process_name Processes.process_id Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | search NOT (parent_process="*HealthMailbox*" OR process="*\\Exchange*\\Bin\\*") | eval reason="w3wp.exe spawned a non-Exchange native tool — Silk Typhoon webshell post-exploit signal" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — w3wp.exe spawning native tools after Exchange webshell drop
let _exchange_safe_children = dynamic(["WerFault.exe","conhost.exe","csc.exe","cvtres.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"                            // IIS worker = Exchange app pool
| where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe",
                      "net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","ipconfig.exe",
                      "systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
                      "mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe",
                      "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe",
                      "regsvr32.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe")                  // Hafnium tool-set
| where FileName !in~ (_exchange_safe_children)
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","Exchange","OWA","ECP","OAB","RpcProxy")  // tied to an Exchange app pool
| extend HafniumIndicator = case(
    FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass", "LSASS dump (T1003.001)",
    FileName in~ ("7z.exe","7za.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any (".7z",".zip","-p"), "Stage-and-archive (T1560.001)",
    FileName =~ "powershell.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-enc","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","Net.WebClient","Invoke-WebRequest"), "Encoded/dl PS (T1059.001)",
    FileName =~ "net.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("group","localgroup","user"), "Discovery (T1087)",
    FileName =~ "certutil.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("-urlcache","-decode"), "Tool transfer (T1105)",
    "Webshell exec")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFolderPath,
          ParentCmd   = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ChildImage  = FolderPath,
          ChildCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
          ChildSHA256 = SHA256,
          IsRemoteSession = InitiatingProcessIntegrityLevel,
          HafniumIndicator
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM — w3wp.exe spawning native tools after Exchange webshell drop (Sentinel / Sysmon-1 or 4688)
let _exchange_safe_children = dynamic(["WerFault.exe","conhost.exe","csc.exe","cvtres.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
| extend ChildName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where tolower(ChildName) in ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","net.exe",
                                "net1.exe","whoami.exe","hostname.exe","ipconfig.exe",
                                "systeminfo.exe","nltest.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
                                "mshta.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe",
                                "procdump.exe","procdump64.exe","rundll32.exe",
                                "regsvr32.exe","7z.exe","7za.exe")
| where ChildName !in (_exchange_safe_children)
| extend HafniumIndicator = case(
    tolower(ChildName) in ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and CommandLine has "lsass", "LSASS dump (T1003.001)",
    tolower(ChildName) in ("7z.exe","7za.exe") and CommandLine has_any (".7z",".zip","-p"), "Stage-and-archive (T1560.001)",
    tolower(ChildName) =~ "powershell.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("-enc","-EncodedCommand","FromBase64String","Net.WebClient","Invoke-WebRequest"), "Encoded/dl PS (T1059.001)",
    tolower(ChildName) =~ "net.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("group","localgroup","user"), "Discovery (T1087)",
    tolower(ChildName) =~ "certutil.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("-urlcache","-decode"), "Tool transfer (T1105)",
    "Webshell exec")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, ParentProcessName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          TokenElevationType, HafniumIndicator
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) IIS Worker Spawning Native Tools
id: 7c2a9d1b-8e34-4f51-9c0a-aaf1c4d7bc11
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects Exchange/IIS w3wp.exe launching native Windows utilities, the canonical post-ProxyLogon webshell execution
  pattern attributed to Silk Typhoon / HAFNIUM (G0125). Pair with the file-create webshell-drop rule for full chain.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.003
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\w3wp.exe'
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\net1.exe'
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\hostname.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\nltest.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\procdump.exe'
      - '\procdump64.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\7z.exe'
      - '\7za.exe'
  filter_benign:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\WerFault.exe'
      - '\conhost.exe'
      - '\csc.exe'
      - '\cvtres.exe'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_child and not filter_benign
falsepositives:
  - Custom Exchange management scripts run by SCOM/monitoring under the Exchange app-pool identity
  - Microsoft cumulative update / hotfix install routines that briefly recycle w3wp
level: high
ucs.rationale: w3wp.exe is the IIS worker process that hosts the Exchange OWA/ECP/OAB app pools. In a healthy Exchange estate it never legitimately spawns cmd.exe, PowerShell, net.exe, certutil.exe or procdump — it only spawns Exchange-internal helpers (csc.exe for ASPX compile, WerFault, conhost). Silk Typhoon's signature ProxyLogon kill chain (and follow-on 2023-25 Exchange/SharePoint zero-days they are still

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon — China Chopper-style webshell drop into Exchange virtual directories
ucs.description: Catches the file-create event that precedes UC1: w3wp.exe (or UMWorkerProcess.exe for CVE-2021-26857) writing a .aspx/.ashx/.asmx file into the Exchange OWA / ECP / aspnet_client virtual paths. This is the exact ProxyLogon → CVE-2021-27065 file-write primitive Silk Typhoon used to plant 'help.aspx', 'shell.aspx', 'supp0rt.aspx' and the unique 8-character random China Chopper variants documented by Microsoft DART and Volexity.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe") AND Filesystem.action="created" AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\ecp\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\Exchange Server\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asmx") by host Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | search NOT file_name IN ("errorFE.aspx","logoff.aspx","logon.aspx","signout.aspx","OutlookSession.aspx","RedirSuiteServiceProxy.aspx") | eval reason="IIS process wrote .aspx into Exchange virtual dir — Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon webshell drop" | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// Silk Typhoon — webshell file drop into Exchange virtual directories (post CVE-2021-27065 / ProxyShell / CVE-2024 series)
let _benign_aspx = dynamic([
    "errorFE.aspx","logoff.aspx","logon.aspx","signout.aspx",
    "OutlookSession.aspx","RedirSuiteServiceProxy.aspx","exporttool.aspx"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where ActionType == "FileCreated"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","MSExchangeMailboxAssistants.exe")
| where FolderPath has_any (
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\",
    @"\ClientAccess\Owa\",
    @"\ClientAccess\ecp\",
    @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\")
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asmx"
| where FileName !in~ (_benign_aspx)
| extend HafniumPattern = case(
    FileName matches regex @"(?i)^[a-z0-9]{8}\.aspx$", "China Chopper 8-char random (Volexity IOC)",
    FileName has_any ("help","shell","supp0rt","aspnet","errorEE","errorEEE","errorEW","errorFF","healthcheck","OutlookEN"), "Microsoft DART named-shell IOC",
    "Unknown ASPX in Exchange vdir")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp between (ago(180d) .. ago(30d))
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | summarize by FileName, FolderPath
  ) on FileName, FolderPath
| where isempty(FileName1)                                                  // first-time-seen filename in this vdir
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName,
          InitiatingProcessCommandLine, HafniumPattern
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Silk Typhoon — webshell drop into Exchange virtual paths (Sentinel ASIM)
let _benign_aspx = dynamic([
    "errorFE.aspx","logoff.aspx","logon.aspx","signout.aspx",
    "OutlookSession.aspx","RedirSuiteServiceProxy.aspx","exporttool.aspx"]);
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventType == "FileCreated"
| where ActingProcessName has_any ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","MSExchangeMailboxAssistants.exe")
| where TargetFilePath has_any (
    @"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\",
    @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\",
    @"\ClientAccess\Owa\",
    @"\ClientAccess\ecp\",
    @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\")
| where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asmx"
| where TargetFileName !in~ (_benign_aspx)
| extend HafniumPattern = case(
    TargetFileName matches regex @"(?i)^[a-z0-9]{8}\.aspx$", "China Chopper 8-char random (Volexity IOC)",
    TargetFileName has_any ("help","shell","supp0rt","aspnet","errorEE","errorEEE","errorEW","errorFF","healthcheck","OutlookEN"), "Microsoft DART named-shell IOC",
    "Unknown ASPX in Exchange vdir")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, ActingProcessName,
          TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256, HafniumPattern
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) China Chopper Webshell Drop in Exchange Virtual Directory
id: 1f6d4a82-2c5b-49a6-bd0a-9e8c9b3a2d77
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects creation of .aspx / .ashx / .asmx files inside Exchange OWA, ECP, or aspnet_client virtual directories
  by w3wp.exe, UMWorkerProcess.exe, or other IIS / Exchange service processes. Mirrors Microsoft DART and Volexity
  IOCs for Silk Typhoon's ProxyLogon (CVE-2021-26855/-26857/-26858/-27065) and ProxyShell drops, including the
  characteristic 8-character random filename China Chopper variant.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://www.volexity.com/blog/2021/03/02/active-exploitation-of-microsoft-exchange-zero-day-vulnerabilities/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/07
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.g0125
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\UMWorkerProcess.exe'
      - '\MSExchangeMailboxAssistants.exe'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\'
      - '\ClientAccess\Owa\'
      - '\ClientAccess\ecp\'
      - '\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\'
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.asmx'
  filter_benign_names:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\errorFE.aspx'
      - '\logoff.aspx'
      - '\logon.aspx'
      - '\signout.aspx'
      - '\OutlookSession.aspx'
      - '\RedirSuiteServiceProxy.aspx'
      - '\exporttool.aspx'
  condition: selection_proc and selection_path and selection_ext and not filter_benign_names
falsepositives:
  - Exchange Cumulative Update install writing legitimate ASPX (correlate against KB install events on the host)
  - Custom in-house OWA branding extensions deployed via Exchange management shell
level: high
ucs.rationale: These exact paths and file extensions are the file-write sink of the CVE-2021-27065 post-auth arbitrary-file-write primitive that Silk Typhoon chained off the CVE-2021-26855 SSRF. Microsoft DART, Volexity, and CISA AA21-062A all enumerated identical IOC paths (\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\, \HttpProxy\owa\auth\, \HttpProxy\ecp\auth\) and named webshells (help.aspx, supp0rt.aspx, plus 8-char rand
